2605.27299.title: Risk Averse Alert Prioritization for IDS Using Subnormal Gaussian Fuzzy Models
2605.27299.paper_summary: Modern intrusion detection systems generate thousands of alerts daily, but alert fatigue severely limits security operations effectiveness due to too many false positives or low-impact events. We address this by proposing a principled framework for alert prioritization based on subnormal Gaussian fuzzy numbers, explicitly modeling three sources of uncertainty: threat severity, detection confidence, and organizational risk attitude. Each alert is represented as a fuzzy number with the core indicating severity, spread indicating uncertainty, and height reflecting detection reliability. We apply ranking indices to prioritize alerts, allowing organizations to tune security posture through a risk-attitude parameter. Experimental validation on CIC-IDS2017 and NSL-KDD demonstrates greater robustness than baselines under detector degradation (0.9963 vs 0.8215 NDCGrel@100), with distinct differentiation in mid-confidence alerts and near-parity with baselines under robust detectors. The framework is theoretically grounded, computationally efficient, provides interpretable reasoning, and remains robust across detector families and miscalibration scenarios.
2605.27299.paper_summary_zh: 現代入侵檢測系統每天產生數千條警報，但由於過多的假陽性或低影響事件，警報疲勞嚴重限制了安全操作的有效性。為此，我們提出了一個基於亞常態高斯模糊數的警報優先級排序的原則框架，明確建模三種不確定性來源：威脅嚴重性、檢測信心和組織風險態度。每個警報被表示為一個模糊數，其核心表示嚴重性，擴散表示不確定性，高度反映檢測可靠性。我們應用排名指數來優先排序警報，使組織能夠通過風險態度參數調整安全姿態。在CIC-IDS2017和NSL-KDD上的實驗驗證顯示，在檢測器降級下比基準更具穩健性（0.9963對0.8215 NDCGrel@100），在中等信心警報中有明顯的區分，並在穩健檢測器下與基準接近平行。該框架在理論上是有根據的，計算上高效，提供可解釋的推理，並在不同檢測器系列和錯誤校準情境中保持穩健性。
2605.27299.author: Murat Moran et.al.
